zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Agnes Clarke於1507年出生在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利時，她的父親Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer22歲，母親Elizabeth De Ferrers27歲。她於1520年與Simon Stone在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1578年7月在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Agnes Clarke于1507年出生在英国英格兰艾可格亞特 布羅姆利时，她的父亲Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer22岁，母亲Elizabeth De Ferrers27岁。她于1520年与Simon Stone在英国英格兰艾可格亞特 布羅姆利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1578年7月在英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Agnes Clarke was born in 1507, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer, was 22 and her mother, Elizabeth De Ferrers, was 27. She married Simon Stone in 1520, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in July 1578, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, at the age of 71.

Elle a épousé Simon Stone en 1520, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en juillet 1578, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans.

ko.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Clarke(이)가 1507년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth De Ferrers(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 Simon Stone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1578년 7월 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Agnes Clarke nasceu em 1507, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth De Ferrers, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Simon Stone em 1520, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em julho de 1578, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, com 71 anos.

ru.Barton St. David, Somerset, England: бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Agnes Clarke родилась в 1507 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth De Ferrers, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Simon Stone в 1520 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла июль 1578 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Agnes Clarke nació en 1507, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir  James Clerke Baron of the Exchquer, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Elizabeth De Ferrers, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Simon Stone en 1520, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en julio de 1578, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años.
